abstract.para: &cockpit; allows you to view and remove the applications installed in &productname;.

topic.para: The Applications tab lists the modules enabled in the &cockpit; web interface side pane. Adding an application adds a new page to the sidebar that allows you to manage a complex system service (like Firewalls or Virtual Machines) through a visual interface instead of the command line. The applications such as Networking, Packages, Repositories, SELinux, and so on appear in the Applications page. If a service fails to start, the Applications page shows you the specific systemd error log on the screen.
To view the list of installed applications, click Applications on the left pane. When you click an application name, the web interface displays the corresponding application tab.
If the Applications tab is empty or if any module name is missing, run the following command to refresh the packages
To remove the application from &cockpit; click the Remove button corresponding to the application.
